creator: jbeich@FreeBSD.org
text:
Why? Binary packages are designed to be maximally useful aka "batteries included". Some Wayland contributors (e.g., Greg V) use PulseAudio. AFAIK, PulseAudio API is also supported by PipeWire[1]. Native audio API on FreeBSD is OSSv4 but it's completely a different version[2] than what can be tested on Linux[3] and not supported by Waybar directly. Both PulseAudio *and* sndio can be undesired, that's why the port has options to select before build.

[1] Not tested myself. I've only used PipeWire for video-only screen sharing in Kooha and Firefox on FreeBSD.
[2] https://wiki.freebsd.org/Sound
    https://people.freebsd.org/~ariff/SOUND_4.TXT.html (see OSSv4 Compatibility)
[3] https://www.kernel.org/doc/html/latest/sound/designs/oss-emulation.html
    http://opensound.com/ (also available as audio/oss port on FreeBSD)
